page: Amazon Web Services c6id.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores based on the x86_64 Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. The system includes 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. It is equipped with 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage and supports a baseline network bandwidth of 38 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU and Geekbench multi-core scores placing in the top 25%. The processor's instruction set, including AVX-512 and cryptography extensions, contributes to top-tier performance in Passmark encryption and image processing tests, both ranking in the top 10%. Memory bandwidth is also highly rated, with uncached large-block reads reaching 326.43 GB/sec. Single-core performance and text processing benchmarks remain in the average tier.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c6id.24xlarge offers high resource density for compute-bound workloads by pairing dedicated Intel Xeon cores with high-capacity local NVMe storage. The inclusion of 5.7 TB of direct-attached NVMe SSDs reduces the need for external block storage, optimizing costs for data-intensive operations. However, the 2.0 GB of memory per core represents a tradeoff for memory-bound applications, which may require scaling to memory-optimized families instead.

This instance is suited for high-throughput database operations, multi-threaded data processing, and static web serving, supported by its strong Redis SET performance and 66.98 GB/sec web serving throughput. It is also appropriate for software compilation, ray tracing, and moderate-scale LLM inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services c6id.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. It is configured with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 38 Gbps. Benchmark results show strong multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and top-tier capabilities in encryption and image processing. While single-core performance is average, its high-density compute and fast local storage make it highly cost-efficient for workloads that require rapid local data access without relying on external network storage. The c6id.24xlarge is well-suited for database operations, multi-threaded software compilation, static web serving, and data-intensive compute tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6id.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6id.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 96 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 5.7 TB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A high-density compute instance pairing ninety-six Intel Xeon vCPUs with five point seven terabytes of fast local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 48 physical Intel Xeon 8375C cores running at 3.5 GHz
192 GB of DDR4 memory and 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Strong multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database performance
38 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the Nitro hypervisor
Suited for high-throughput databases, software compilation, and web serving
